item.title: Ukraine urges EU to shut down Russia crypto sanctions evasion in 21st package
item.description: Kyiv is pushing Brussels to target cryptocurrency channels Russia uses to evade sanctions as part of the upcoming 21st EU sanctions package. Ukraine's top sanctions official said the measures must "finally shut down channels for using cryptocurrencies to circumvent restrictions."

item.title: NATO picks Saab-Bombardier GlobalEye to replace aging AWACS fleet
item.description: NATO's procurement agency has selected Sweden's Saab and Canada's Bombardier to replace the alliance's Boeing E-3A Sentry AWACS aircraft with the GlobalEye platform, marking the first non-Boeing aircraft to serve as NATO's airborne surveillance backbone since 1982.

item.title: DeepSeek releases V4 AI model in fresh challenge to US competitors
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek unveiled its long-delayed V4 model, intensifying competition among Chinese AI companies and posing a renewed challenge to U.S. players already feeling threatened by Chinese foundation models. Huawei says its Ascend chips "fully support" the model.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire three weeks after White House talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks following talks at the White House. Trump said the U.S. will work with Lebanon to protect it from Hezbollah. Hezbollah was not present at the talks and says it retains "the right to resist."

item.title: Australia invests AB in counter-drone defenses with laser and interceptor systems
item.description: Australia is more than doubling funding for counter-drone defenses to A$7 billion, awarding contracts for AIM Defence's Fractl laser system and Sypaq Systems' Corvo Strike interceptor drone. Both will integrate into the Army's command-and-control system for protecting forces and infrastructure.

item.title: NATO Has No Provision to Expel Members, Spokesperson Says After Spain Suspension Report
item.description: NATO responded to reports of a Pentagon email proposing to suspend Spain over lack of support for the Iran war, clarifying the alliance has no mechanism to expel members. The incident highlights growing tensions between the US and European allies.

item.title: Kenyan President Sparks Diplomatic Outcry After Mocking Nigerians English
item.description: President William Ruto claimed Kenyans speak the best English in the world while mocking Nigerians pronunciation, drawing criticism from Nigerians and diplomatic observers across Africa.

item.title: White House Memo Alleges Systematic AI Model Theft by Chinese Firms
item.description: A memo from Michael Kratsios claims Chinese companies are distilling US AI models through systematic theft, escalating tensions over technology and intellectual property between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Syria arrests key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre from civil war
item.description: Syrian authorities have arrested Amjad Youssef, one of the most-wanted fugitives for the 2013 Tadamon massacre where nearly 290 civilians were killed under the Assad regime. Victims were bound, blindfolded, shot and dumped in a pit.

item.title: Philippines ex-president Duterte to stand trial for crimes against humanity
item.description: The International Criminal Court has confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte over the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs." He will face trial at The Hague.

item.title: Europe debates mutual defense options outside NATO as US commitment wavers
item.description: European Union nations are examining their little-known mutual defense obligation as confidence in US security guarantees erodes. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO but reflects growing European anxiety about American reliability.

item.title: Gaza to hold first local election in nearly two decades amid ongoing war
item.description: Gaza is set to hold its first municipal elections in two decades in Deir al-Balah this weekend. Hamas said it is not participating. Residents see it as a long-awaited opportunity to address the city's infrastructure problems.

item.title: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a hellhole
item.description: India has expressed outrage after US President Donald Trump shared social media posts calling India a "hellhole." The comments have sparked diplomatic tensions between the two nations amid ongoing geopolitical realignments.

item.title: Polands Tusk Questions US Loyalty to NATO Alliance
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk told the Financial Times that Europes biggest question is whether the United States remains committed to NATO treaty obligations, highlighting growing anxiety about transatlantic security commitments.

item.title: Ukraine First Country to Scale Remotely Operated Interceptor Drones
item.description: Ukraine has become the first nation to scale the use of remotely operated interceptor drones capable of long-distance strikes, Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov announced, marking a significant advancement in drone warfare technology.

item.title: China Places EU Defense and Aerospace Firms on Export Control List
item.description: Beijing barred seven EU defense and aerospace entities from receiving dual-use items originating in China, escalating trade tensions amid the ongoing Iran conflict and Western sanctions regime.

item.title: DeepSeek Unveils New AI Model as Huawei Pledges Full Chip Support
item.description: Chinese AI company DeepSeek released its cost-efficient V4 model that competes with top closed-source models from OpenAI and Google, while Huawei vowed full support with new chips, intensifying the US-China AI race.

item.title: Iran War Has Significantly Depleted US Ammunition Stockpiles
item.description: The ongoing conflict with Iran has significantly depleted US ammunition stockpiles, raising concerns about American military readiness and the sustainability of prolonged engagements in the Middle East.

item.title: Thailand to Put 44 Opposition Figures on Trial Over Royal Insult Law Reform Bid
item.description: The Supreme Court ordered trial for current and former lawmakers from the People's Party and dissolved Move Forward party who attempted to amend the lese-majeste law, facing potential lifetime bans from office.

item.title: US Says Iran Can Play at World Cup but Bars Those With IRGC Ties
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed Iranian players can participate in the 2026 World Cup hosted in the US, but individuals with connections to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps will be denied entry.

item.title: Iran Rejects Trump Leadership Rift Claims, Presents Unified Front
item.description: Iranian officials including President Pezeshkian and Foreign Minister Araghchi dismissed Trump assertions of internal divisions, emphasizing iron unity and complete obedience to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: EU Unblocks €90 Billion Ukraine Loan, Approves New Russia Sanctions
item.description: The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine and a 20th round of sanctions on Russia after Hungary and Slovakia dropped objections following Ukraine restoration of Russian oil flows.

item.title: Japan Deploys Long-Range Missiles in Southern Shield Buildup Against China
item.description: Japan has deployed long-range missiles to Kyushu as part of a southern defense strategy, citing its most severe security environment since 1945 and growing concerns about US commitment to regional defense.

item.title: Philippines Ex-President Duterte to Stand Trial at ICC for Crimes Against Humanity
item.description: The International Criminal Court has confirmed that former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte will stand trial for extrajudicial killings during his war on drugs, which killed thousands.

item.title: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs, 10 Percent of Workforce, While Microsoft Offers Buyouts
item.description: Meta announced its largest layoff since 2023, cutting one in ten employees after spending billions on AI development, while Microsoft offers voluntary buyouts to 8,750 workers.
